rationale: Educ. Code §25.038 governs tuition fees for transfer students and references state aid under §25.037. It does not reference homestead exemptions, Tax Code §11.13, or property tax appraisal values. No direct conflict exists.
quote: The receiving school district may charge a tuition fee to the extent that the district's actual expenditure per student in average daily attendance, as determined by its board of trustees, exceeds the sum the district benefits from state aid sources as provided by Section 25.037.
_note: No direct textual conflict. Any impact on school district finances from the homestead exemption change would be indirect through state aid formulas, not through this tuition-fee statute.
